seoTitle: Password Protect GoHighLevel Pages and Websites
metaDescription: Restrict access to private GoHighLevel pages by enabling password protection for membership content and exclusive resources.
intro: Restricting access to certain pages is essential when you're offering membership content, client resources, or exclusive offers through GoHighLevel. This tutorial covers enabling password protection, creating login credentials, and managing who can view your gated content.

steps.heading: Open page or website settings
steps.body: Navigate to the settings for the specific page or website you want to protect within GoHighLevel.

steps.heading: Enable password protection
steps.body: Turn on password protection for the page so visitors need credentials to view the content.

steps.heading: Configure access and save changes
steps.body: Set up the access credentials and save your settings so the protection takes effect.

steps.heading: Test the protected page
steps.body: Visit the page yourself to confirm the password prompt appears and that entering the correct credentials grants access.

steps.heading: Manage ongoing access
steps.body: Update or share credentials as needed to control who can view your private content over time.

faq.question: Can I password protect a single page instead of the whole site?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers enabling password protection for individual pages and funnels, not just entire websites.

faq.question: What kind of content is this useful for?
faq.answer: The tutorial mentions membership content, client resources, and exclusive offers as common use cases for password protection.

faq.question: How do I create login credentials for visitors?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers creating secure login credentials as part of setting up password protection.

faq.question: Can I test the protected page before sharing it?
faq.answer: Yes, testing the protected page is a specific step covered in the tutorial to confirm it works correctly.

faq.question: Who is this tutorial for?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, business owners, course creators, membership site owners, and GoHighLevel beginners.
